data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine) × child B3 (dc58: completion-verifier as an engine workflow, uses → prompt → uses → uses)]

Walked all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (B1 723c, B2 6b06, B4 101a, B5 f12d, B6 7e48, B-RETIRE 961b, B-DM 5506, B-UX 92fa, 0408). RESULT: CLEAN — no high-confidence finding. The {{plan}}-plumbing inconsistency that the B2/B6/0408 audits flag is, on close inspection, NOT present for this child: it is specific to the plan-review prompts and does not touch the completion-verifier. Each mode footnoted below; all read benign under the anti-FP / benign-reading rule.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Why the headline B2/0408 inconsistency does NOT attach to B3
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The recurring epic-wide defect is: the generic RunnerAgentStep (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py:131–137) supplies ONLY {ticket_id, ticket_context, repo_path} to resolve_prompt, so the plan-review verifier/coach prompts' required {{plan}} (+ findings/surviving listings) are unsupplied → review-plan via workflow degrades to INDETERMINATE (PR #42 reverted gate_engine to bespoke; 0408 open to fix). I verified the completion-verifier prompt does NOT share this gap:
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md interpolates exactly {{ticket_id}} (line 35) and {{ticket_context}} (line 37) — and NOTHING else. No {{plan}}, no findings-listing variable.
- runs.py:131–137 supplies both of those (ticket_id + ticket_context) plus repo_path — so the completion-verifier prompt resolves with no undefined variable on the LIVE path.
- B3's own assumption (comment 1782423637816890001: "the engine AUTO-INJECTS ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path for prompt steps, so the verify step needs no 'with'") is CORRECT for this prompt — it was the plan-review migration (B2) that wrongly inherited it, not B3 that propagated a defect.
- The graph-aware ticket_context is genuinely supplied: gate_ops.completion_precheck (gate_ops.py:69–84) assembles + fences the context (operations._assemble_context, graph=True for epics) and the verify step consumes it — so the completion gate is self-contained and live-runnable. B5's signed completion-verification PASS confirms produce_completion_verdict works live and the close gate fail-closes on outage. So B3's live-runnability is established, not deferred to a 0408-style follow-up.
Conclusion: B3 is live-correct on the workflow path (signing is the only deferred piece, and that deferral is by-design + roster-covered — see Mode 6); the {{plan}} live blind-spot is a plan-review-only artifact and is NOT a B3 consistency finding.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The 7 modes — all benign:

(1) Implicit shared state — B3 authors gates/completion-verification.yaml + gate_ops.py (completion_precheck/reconcile/passthrough). B5 (f12d) later READS these at the close gate and lands the engine fix (_resolve_terminal_output descends a terminal branch into the taken arm's leaf, executor.py:497–515) so RunResult.terminal_output surfaces the branch-arm completion_verdict — a concern B3 itself flagged for B5 (f12d comment 1782424841855352001). This is SEQUENCED (B5 after B4 after B3) and acknowledged on B3's face, not hidden concurrent mutation. Benign.

(2) Conflicting assumptions — B3's "engine auto-injects → verify needs no with:" assumption is CORRECT for the completion-verifier prompt (verified above); it does NOT conflict with the live tree (unlike B2). B3's branch-vs-bare-if: redesign (comment 1782423637816890001: referencing an if:-SKIPPED step's outputs raises, so a branch whose arms reference only in-arm steps is the faithful short-circuit) is internally consistent and is the very pattern B2 (6b06) later reuses per ADR 0003. No conflict. Benign.

(3) Dependency gap — B3 depends only on existing step kinds (no new constructs, no epic-A constructs, no B1 batch-runner) — it is explicitly the walking-skeleton/simplest migration. It reuses completion.py helpers (_child_closure_findings/_deterministic_child_failure/_reconcile) which B-RETIRE (961b) scopes itself to plan-review only and does NOT remove — so B3's reused helpers survive retirement. B3 blocks B4 (101a) (dep present: dc58 blocks 101a), which needs B3 to exist to diff. No missing dependency. Benign.

(4) Scope overlap — B3 owns the completion-verification workflow authoring; B4 (101a) READS its planned trace for parity; B5 (f12d) CONSUMES the YAML at the close gate + defers signing; B-DM (5506)/B-UX (92fa) build the editor over the same prompt/criteria library. Each is a distinct layer over B3's artifact, sequenced, not duplicated ownership. Notably, the completion-verification gate has NO bespoke-vs-workflow dual path to retire (B-RETIRE is plan-review-only), consistent with B3 being the simpler migration. Benign.

(5) Ordering violation — B3 is correctly BEFORE B4 (parity needs B3 to exist) and B5 (cutover needs parity). Deps: dc58 blocks 101a; 101a blocks f12d. Signing is deferred to B5 (the close-gate owner), which is the correct downstream owner since completion.py has no signing helper. Consistent. Benign.

(6) Consumer impact — B3 is purely additive/offline expand-contract: "the existing completion.py call path stays the live gate; cutover/removal is B5." It does not change the live close gate at its own boundary, so no consumer break at B3's boundary. The deferred signing is correctly owned by B5 (which preserves byte-compatible completion_verdict HMAC signing) and is roster-covered, so the deferral leaves no orphaned consumer. (The branch-arm verdict-surfacing wrinkle B3 flagged is resolved by B5's executor fix — a downstream completion of B3's consumption contract, not an unhandled break.) Benign.

(7) Residual references — B3 references the existing completion-verifier prompt + completion_verdict schema + completion.py helpers (all present); it removes nothing. completion.py stays intact (expand-contract). No dangling reference to a removed symbol. Benign.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ANTI-FP note: The epic-wide {{plan}} inconsistency (flagged on B2 6b06 and B6 7e48, owned by open task 0408) was the most plausible candidate finding for this pairing, since B3 originated the "engine auto-injects" assumption that B2 mis-inherited. I deliberately checked it against the live tree and the completion_verifier.md prompt and confirmed it does NOT attach to B3: the completion-verifier needs only {{ticket_id}}+{{ticket_context}}, both supplied by runs.py:131–137, and the graph-aware context is assembled by completion_precheck — so B3's completion-verification workflow is live-correct (corroborated by B5's signed PASS of produce_completion_verdict). Reporting a {{plan}}-style gap on B3 would be a false positive. No other mode rises to high confidence. Clean pairing — empty findings list.
